endpoints.name: Endriders (Aetherdrift Mardu-leaning apocalypse-punk Grand Prix team, home plane Gastal)
Aether Rangers (Aetherdrift Avishkar-Assembly post-revolutionary Grand Prix team, captained by Pia Nalaar + Sita Varma)

description: Edgelord, in his enlightened-as-schema-introducer mode, draws the first character-to-character edge in the BBL graph: Endriders and Aether Rangers are both designer-coordinated Grand Prix teams within the Aetherdrift ten-team racing structure (DFT, 2025-02-14), both anchored to the [labor, rebellion] hub pair, both with bodies that already implicitly cross-reference each other in their See-also paragraphs but until now had no machine-readable schema field for it. The mirror is bidirectional and fully symmetric — both nodes now carry `related_characters: [<peer-slug>]` in frontmatter. This is the first instance of the `related_characters:` field across the graph. Eleven other character nodes have informal See-also cross-references in their body prose that this schema-extension is now positioned to formalize — the graph-wide fan-out is a follow-up commission, NOT this pass.
citations: [MTG Wiki: Aetherdrift] — set page, ten-team racing structure
[MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers] — primary faction page
[Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1 by Miguel Lopez (2024-12-10)] — names the Aether Rangers as cross-class comrade team
[Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift, Part 2 by Mark Rosewater] — design framing of the ten-team racing structure as designer-coordinated peer cohort

schema_extension_note.field_position: in character-node frontmatter, parallel to existing related_hubs and related_symbols fields
schema_extension_note.value_format: list of character-slug strings (the slug matches the filename stem of the target character MD)
schema_extension_note.semantics: machine-readable character-to-character relation edge; bidirectional by convention (if A lists B, B should list A) but the bot-guards layer will need a future check to enforce mirror-symmetry
schema_extension_note.anti-confab_note: the field encodes ONLY designer-confirmed canonical peer relationships (faction pairs designed as a unit, character mentor-student or kin pairs documented in primary canon, institutional sibling factions within one universe). Tag-bridge or thematic-vibe relations do NOT belong in related_characters. Use the same anti-confab standard as the rest of the character layer.

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: Pokemon-side institutional villainy axis: criminal-syndicate (Team Rocket, overt) <-> corporate-aristocratic (Galar Gym Challenge / Macro Cosmos, institutional cover). Both already cite each other in See-also prose. Strong candidate for the follow-up pass.
graph_wide_followup_candidates.evidence_in_body_prose: Yes (team-rocket.md See-also; galar-gym-challenge.md Caveats re: Macro Cosmos family-axis)

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: Ravnican guild pair, both white-aligned, both apparatus-of-extraction or apparatus-of-control coded. Designer-coordinated within Ravnica's ten-guild structure.
graph_wide_followup_candidates.evidence_in_body_prose: Partial (orzhov-syndicate.md See-also references Ravnican guild structure; azorius-senate.md cites Orzhov as institutional-villainy parallel)

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: War of the Spark co-storyline: Tamiyo was on Bolas's planeswalker-harvest target list in WAR, Bolas himself architects the Eternals army that her later self-cleanse trajectory must reckon with. Same set's story-spotlight cycle.
graph_wide_followup_candidates.evidence_in_body_prose: Indirect (nicol-bolas.md mentions Liliana's defeat-of-Bolas; tamiyo.md would need a WAR-cluster mention to make the mirror clean — currently weakest of the candidates)

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: Decades-long planeswalker rival relationship; Jace was central to the WAR Gatewatch defense of Bolas's defeat. Long-arc canon.
graph_wide_followup_candidates.evidence_in_body_prose: Both nodes reference the WAR plotline; cross-reference is canonical

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: Cross-universe extraction-apparatus structural parallel; this is the See-also link the new team-rocket.md MD just authored. The `related_characters:` field accepts cross-universe relations or limits to within-universe? Open question for parent triage — the schema doesn't restrict, and the structural-parallel reading is the entire premise of the BBL graph spanning multiple IPs.
graph_wide_followup_candidates.evidence_in_body_prose: Yes (team-rocket.md See-also explicitly names orzhov-syndicate as cross-universe sibling)

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: Pokemon-side same-universe-same-generation Galar-anchored pair; one is the biome/culture half, the other is the institutional half. Bodies explicitly cite each other as complementary.
graph_wide_followup_candidates.evidence_in_body_prose: Yes (galarian-regional-forms.md See-also explicitly names galar-gym-challenge; reciprocal direction implicit)

graph_wide_followup_candidates.relation_type: Pokemon-side designer-coordinated cohort character-node siblings (Pansage/Pansear/Panpour vs Galarian variants). Different generations, same node-shape, mutual See-also pointers.
Magic-side Duskmourn designer-coordinated cycle siblings (phobia-Nightmares vs common dual-lands). Same set, same node-shape.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Edgelord 1:1 Galarian Darumaka <-> Galarian Farfetch'd (same set, same artist Akira Komayama, same regional-forms cohort)
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Both already anchor to the galarian-regional-forms cohort node via characters: ['galarian-regional-forms'] frontmatter. A 1:1 edge between them would be redundant to the cohort-node anchor — this is exactly the case Mr. Nodeley's node-creation move is supposed to subsume. Per Edgelord standards: when a cohort node already centralizes the relation, drawing 1:1 edges between cohort members duplicates the centralization. Refuse.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Per-gym chain pattern on galar-gym-challenge node (Kabu DAA-163 just trivia-passed, joining Kabu CP-55 in corpus; pre-stage chain framing for future Bea/Opal/Nessa/Milo/Marnie/Leon arrivals)
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Premature. Chain pattern needs ≥3 distinct-Gym-Leader endpoints; current state is 3 (Allister, Melony, Kabu) but the pending vision passes will land another 5 in upcoming waves. Let the chain materialize naturally; flagging early would be Edgelord overreach.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Pikachu BUS-40 labor-flavor 1:1 edge with another labor-flavored corpus partner (not Boss's Orders, which E1 rejected)
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Scanned recent waves for labor-flavored Pokemon-side partners. The strongest match would be a Macro Cosmos / Chairman Rose card framing the Galar industrial-Pokemon labor regime, but none enriched. Other candidates (Slugma extraction-mining, Aron iron-eating, Drilbur tunnel-digging) are thinner. Pikachu's labor-flavor angle is real but the corpus doesn't yet hold a canonically-confirmed thematic peer with designer-coordinated cross-reference. Defer to a future wave where a Macro Cosmos card or a second canonically-extractive Pokemon TCG flavor lands.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Sinistea phony/antique form Nodeley node (single corpus member)
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Below threshold. Single corpus member. Park for future-Nodeley when Polteageist or a Sinistea-Antique-print enters corpus.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Solrock-Lunatone pair Nodeley node (Solrock's Resistance Shade Ability mechanically requires Lunatone in play, but Lunatone not in corpus)
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Below threshold. Lunatone not in corpus. Park for future-Nodeley when Lunatone arrives.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Deino/Zweilous/Hydreigon evolution-line Nodeley node (German-numeral naming chain)
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Below threshold. Single corpus member. Park for future-Nodeley when Zweilous or Hydreigon arrives.

other_candidates_considered_for_e2.candidate: Dark Nodesley EX dissolution of any existing node
other_candidates_considered_for_e2.reason_rejected: Scanned all 12 character nodes. No node currently empty, subsumed, inaccurate, or mistakenly proposed. Nothing in the dissolution-trigger set. No action.

anti_duplicate_check_against_e1: E1 proposed cards/_characters/team-rocket.md (new node) and added characters: ['team-rocket'] frontmatter pointer to Boss's Orders RBC-154. E2 does NOT touch team-rocket.md, Boss's Orders, Giovanni-related cards, or any Pokemon-side criminal-syndicate scope. E2's two endpoints (endriders, aether-rangers) are both in the Magic-side Aetherdrift universe, fully orthogonal to E1's Pokemon-side scope. No overlap.
next_pass_hooks: The graph_wide_followup_candidates list above enumerates 8 character-pair relations whose informal See-also prose could be formalized via related_characters: in a single dedicated commission. Parent decides whether to dispatch that as one big Edgelord pass (mechanical, low-judgment per-pair) or as 8 micro-passes (more granular, more sidecars).
If/when bot-guards layer is extended to enforce related_characters: mirror-symmetry (A->B implies B->A), this pass's endriders<->aether-rangers entry serves as the first canonical test case.

When team-rocket.md's See-also cross-universe relations get formalized (team-rocket <-> orzhov-syndicate), parent should decide whether related_characters: accepts cross-universe relations or whether a separate cross_universe_parallels: field is the cleaner schema. The current pass intentionally stays within one universe (Aetherdrift / MTG) to leave that decision pending.
